pulse: Healthcare sector median valuations currently sit at 13.7x EV/EBITDA and 25.5x P/E, reflecting competitive positioning relative to the broader market. Notably, Gilead (GILD) reported flat quarterly profits but raised its full-year outlook primarily due to strong HIV sales, aligning with the trend of resilient pharmaceutical demand highlighted in Bloomberg’s report that health-care earnings are outperforming the S&P 500. For investors, the current positive momentum in pharmaceutical stocks, driven by robust earnings like those from AstraZeneca (AZN) as they maintain forecasts bolstered by cancer and heart drugs, suggests a potential breakout opportunity in the sector as market fundamentals improve.
